title: Roldana hirsuticaulis
taxonomy: Roldana hirsuticaulis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Roldana, which is a group of plants native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The genus Roldana is further classified within the tribe Madieae, a group of plants characterized by their daisy-like flowers. Roldana hirsuticaulis is a member of the subtribe Madinae, which includes plants with hairy stems and leaves. The species is also known to be closely related to other plants in the genus, such as Roldana petasites and Roldana rupicola.
uses: Roldana hirsuticaulis has been traditionally used in folk medicine in Mexico, where it is valued for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and stems have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, Roldana hirsuticaulis is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. The plant's leaves are also used to make a tea that is thought to aid in digestion and reduce stress. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Roldana hirsuticaulis for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Roldana hirsuticaulis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its leaves and stems contain various compounds that may help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. Additionally, Roldana hirsuticaulis may exhibit antimicrobial properties, which could aid in preventing infections and promoting wound healing. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health and to confirm its efficacy. The plant's health benefits are largely based on traditional knowledge and may require further scientific study for validation.

Roldana hirsuticaulis is also believed to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.  Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of this plant.

intro: Roldana hirsuticaulis, also known as the hairy Mexican goldleaf, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as wound healing, skin infections, and digestive problems. Bioactive constituents of Roldana hirsuticaulis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
